items.title: How to Use Claude Code Agent Teams in 13 Mins (Opus 4.6)
items.description: Build agentic systems that run your business: https://skool.com/scrapes Don't miss the next build ...
extracted_patterns: Use agent teams instead of single agents for projects where context window degradation becomes critical (>95% fill causes compression and information loss)
Decompose complex work into specialized sub-agents to improve output quality (hire specialists, not generalists) and reduce token cost
Execute non-dependent tasks in parallel using separate agent instances instead of sequential execution
Avoid hub-and-spoke architecture (main agent as bottleneck) by using agent teams with shared task lists enabling peer-to-peer agent communication
Spawn autonomous teammate agents from a coordinator that manages a shared task list, allowing agents to claim and complete work independently
Use sub-agents for single-direction reporting; use agent teams when agents need to communicate and iterate with each other (e.g., researcher â†’ reviewer â†’ writer)
Each agent in a team maintains its own context window, preventing context bleeding and improving focus on domain-specific work
Task lists act as the coordination layerâ€”agents pull work, update status, and unblock dependent tasks without routing through a central orchestrator

items.title: Claude Stitch (Design Mode): Anthropic just CRUSHED Google with their DESIGN AGENT!
items.description: In this video, I'll be talking about whether you really need Google Stitch to build great UIs, or whether Verdent plus Claude Opus ...
extracted_patterns: Use plan-first mode for UI builds: Have the model think through structure, components, responsive strategy, and typography before generating code

Design direction via three-part specification: Provide visual thesis (premium/editorial/technical/playful/calm/dense/sparse), content plan (sections and purposes), and interaction thesis (2-3 motion ideas) before requesting builds

Iterative refinement over regeneration: Instead of discarding and starting over, give incremental feedback to refine results (e.g., 'remove cards, make hero more imageled, reduce copy 30%, calmer accent')
Leverage codebase context: Use model's access to actual project structure, existing components, and constraints to make design decisions that fit the product instead of generic patterns
Model selection by task: Claude Opus 4.6 for production UI code (not for quick mockupsâ€”use Stitch for that)

Skill-based art direction: Use front-end design skill as an art direction layer that biases toward strong composition, hierarchy, sparse copy, and prevents common failures (generic cards, cluttered heroes, weak branding)
